title: Seller wants too high price
prompt: The seller insists on listing 12% above your CMA because a neighbor 'got that number.' Walk them down to a defensible list price using comps and days-on-market without losing the listing.

title: Buyer refuses agreement
prompt: A motivated buyer won't sign the OREF buyer representation agreement before you show homes. Explain why it protects them and is now required, and earn the signature.

title: Inspection objection
prompt: Your buyer's inspection found a roof issue. The listing agent is resistant to repairs. Negotiate a repair credit or addendum that keeps the deal alive before the contingency expires.

title: Commission objection
prompt: A seller asks you to cut your commission to match a discount brokerage. Defend your value with marketing, negotiation, and net-proceeds math — without getting defensive.

title: Cash offer explanation
prompt: A homeowner is tempted by an instant cash offer. Walk them through the net-sheet comparison vs a traditional sale so they choose with clear eyes.

title: Multiple-offer strategy
prompt: Your listing has 4 offers. Coach the seller through escalation clauses, financing strength, and close-date trade-offs to pick the best — not just the highest — offer.
